blocks.content:
Q4 Competitive Analysis Framework

Goal: map the ed-consulting landscape before finalizing service offerings

Categories:
- Big firms (Pearson advisory, HMH consulting)
- Boutique shops (Instruction Partners, UnboundEd, Leading Educators)
- Solo consultants (need to pull from LinkedIn, conference speaker lists)

What to look at:
- Services offered
- Pricing (where I can find it)
- Geographic focus
- Client size (district enrollment)
- Specialties (curriculum, leadership, equity, SEL)

Start with the 15 firms from the ASCD conference exhibitor list. Cross-reference with CO DOE vendor registry.

Competitive Analysis - Updated 11/18

Instruction Partners
- K-12 literacy and math
- Pricing not public, est $15-25k/engagement
- Mid-large districts, mostly Southeast
- Different niche from mine

UnboundEd
- Equity-centered curriculum
- Workshops $3,500-$7,500, consulting $20k+
- Urban districts nationally
- Overlap with my equity audit idea. Need to differentiate.

Leading Educators
- Teacher leadership development
- Multi-year, $50k+
- Big urban districts only
- Not a direct competitor

Solo consultants (LinkedIn research):
- Most charge $2-5k/day
- Few have structured tiers
- Almost nobody bundles equity audit + curriculum + coaching
- THIS IS THE GAP. Bundle pricing at the mid-market level.

Business Plan Draft v2

Thompson Educational Consulting

Executive Summary
Curriculum design, leadership coaching, equity audits for mid-size districts (5k-25k enrollment). The districts that can't afford Pearson but need more than one person.

Services
- Tier 1: Curriculum Audit ($4,500) - 2 day on-site + report + 30 day follow-up
- Tier 2: Leadership Coaching ($9,500/qtr) - monthly sessions + email support (raised from $8k per Rebecca's feedback)
- Tier 3: Full Engagement ($18,000/yr) - audit + coaching + equity + quarterly check-ins

Target Market
- CO districts first, then regional
- Districts with active literacy or equity initiatives
- Superintendent-level buyers

Year 1 Revenue Targets
- 3-4 Tier 1 = $13,500-$18,000
- 1-2 Tier 2 = $9,500-$19,000
- Stretch: 1 Tier 3 = $18,000
- Y1 total: $30-55k

Open Questions
- When to leave Jefferson? Need 6 months runway minimum
- Health insurance (Claire's plan? COBRA? Marketplace?)
- LLC timing (Rebecca says January is ideal)

Claire reviewed 12/7. Her notes: "Numbers look conservative which is good but you need to pick a launch date. Two years of research is enough."

2026 Goals & Launch Timeline

Same question as last year: when do I actually do this

Goals:
1. Form LLC by end of Q1
2. Finalize business plan by February
3. Austin EdTech conference Jan 18-20 (registered!)
4. Website by March
5. First paying client by June
6. Decision: stay at JMS or transition by end of school year

Y1 revenue target: $35k minimum to cover gap

Claire says "just set a date and do it." She's right.

Meeting Rebecca in person at Austin conf. Need to bring a decision not more questions.

$87k student loans. $5k savings. If this doesn't work...

Should I go full-time?

Greg sent the 5-year leadership commitment email today. Every principal submits a vision plan by March 1. Board using them for budget allocations.

If I submit that plan = telling the district I'm here for 5 years
If I don't submit = Greg notices. He always notices.

Pros of full-time consulting:
- This is what I actually want
- I know the market
- Claire is behind me (mostly)
- I'm 42. If not now when?

Cons:
- $87k student loans
- Mortgage is $3,400/mo
- Zero clients
- Health insurance
- Leaving the literacy initiative feels like abandoning the kids
- Greg would take it personally

Middle path: submit the 5-year plan, keep building on the side, launch in 2-3 years when finances are better

...that's what I said two years ago

Claire: "you need to make a decision"
Rebecca: "at some point you've got to set a date and go"

Am I just afraid?

2026 Garden Plan

Seed Varieties:
Tomatoes: San Marzano, Cherokee Purple, Sweet 100 (cherry)
Peppers: Jalapeno, Serrano, Bell (red and green)
Herbs: Genovese basil, Thai basil, cilantro, oregano, rosemary, thyme
Bonus: Sugar snap peas, zucchini

Planting Schedule (Denver Zone 5b):
- Indoor starts: March 15 (tomatoes, peppers)
- Direct sow after last frost (~May 15): beans, squash, cucumbers
- Herbs can go out early May with protection
- Peas: direct sow late March if ground is workable

Bed Layouts (updated 3/15):
Bed A (raised, south-facing): tomatoes + basil companion planting. San Marzano in back, Cherokee Purple middle, Sweet 100 front edge.
Bed B (west side): peppers. Full sun. Jalapeno and Serrano together, bells on the end.
Bed C (herb spiral): basil, cilantro, oregano, thyme. Rosemary in its own pot (perennial, bring inside).
Bed D (east side, partial shade): peas early season, then zucchini after peas finish

Companion Planting Notes (added 3/15):
- Basil near tomatoes = pest deterrent + better flavor (seriously this works)
- Marigolds around bed borders for aphid control
- Don't put peppers near fennel
- Cilantro bolts fast in Denver heat, succession plant every 3 weeks starting May
- Rosemary and thyme near peppers = beneficial insect habitat

Seed Order: City Floral Garden Center, placed 3/1
$47.85 total. Should arrive by 3/7.

Notes:
- Last avg frost date Denver: May 7-15
- Consider floating row covers for early starts
- Compost pile needs turning before planting
- Koda dug up the pea bed last year. Need better fencing this time.

Pricing Tiers - Draft v1

Working from the competitive analysis. Most solo consultants charge $2-5k/day. Firms range $15-25k per engagement. Gap in the middle for bundled services at the district level.

Draft pricing:

Tier 1 - Curriculum Audit ($4,500)
- 2-day on-site assessment
- Written report with recommendations
- 30-day follow-up call
- Comparable to independent consultants in CO
- Maybe too low? Need to build the portfolio first though

Tier 2 - Leadership Coaching ($8,000/quarter)
- Monthly 90-min sessions
- Email support between sessions
- Assessment tools and frameworks
- Question: quarterly pricing vs per-session?
- Need to ask Rebecca about this

Tier 3 - Full Engagement ($18,000/year)
- Everything from Tier 1 and 2
- Equity audit component
- Quarterly check-ins for one year
- This is the differentiator. Nobody bundles all three.

Open questions:
- Discovery call tier? Free or paid?
- Travel expenses for out-of-state districts?
- Annual pricing for Tier 3 or per-project?
- Ask Rebecca re quarterly vs per-session
- Research CO district consulting budgets specifically

Need to separate these work sessions better

Last night was too close. Editing pricing tiers at 10:45 and Greg emailed about the literacy initiative progress report at 10:50. I replied from the same laptop two minutes after closing the consulting tab. If I had been sharing my screen or if Greg had called instead of emailed that would have been a disaster.

New rules for myself:
- Consulting work on personal laptop only
- Close all consulting tabs before touching district work
- No consulting work after 9pm on weeknights. Greg always emails late.
- Dedicated nights: Mon/Wed catch up on district stuff, Tue/Thu consulting
- Keep Notion and district email in separate browser profiles
